key_insight: AdvancedMathBench shows that frontier LLMs still struggle with advanced mathematical proof construction and, even more, with reliably detecting proof errors.
engineering_relevance: The benchmark provides a more rigorous way to measure reasoning quality in mathematical AI systems, which is useful for developing and validating proof assistants and verifier models.
method: ProverBench: 296 advanced proof-generation problems spanning undergraduate and doctoral qualifying-exam levels.
Automated proof-verification pipeline trained on large-scale expert annotations to score correctness and identify fine-grained proof errors.
VerifierBench: 888 model-generated proof trajectories with expert ground truth for evaluating proof validity judgments and verification rationales.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports strong expert agreement for the verification pipeline and shows challenging results for models, including GPT-5.5-xhigh at 75.8/66.1 on UGD/QE and a best verification Balanced F1 of 65.1.
watch_item: Benchmark performance may depend on the quality and coverage of expert annotations and could still miss subtle mathematical nuances not captured by automated verification.
paper_title: AdvancedMathBench: A Benchmark Suite for Advanced Mathematical Proof Generation and Verification
